evidence: protein_analysis_utils.py: public functions have complete type annotations including return types
IDR/idr_analysis.py: _worker_fn() has no return type annotation; _start(), _stop() on _PredictionWorker have no return type annotations
IDR/idr_analysis.py: _aiupred_cache: dict = {} is typed as bare 'dict' without key/value types; should be dict[str, Any] or dict[str, Optional[module_type]]
IDR/idr_analysis.py: run_disorder_predictions() uses zip(*records) with a comment 'if records else ([], [])' — the type of records is list[tuple[Any, dict]] but not annotated
IDR/nterm_analysis.py: plot_sequence_logo(), plot_identity_heatmap() use '# type: ignore[name-defined]' for matplotlib return type — acceptable given optional dependency
app/app.py: server() function has no return type annotation; Shiny renders lack return annotations
analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py: build_gene_set() -> set[str] and token_match() -> bool are correctly annotated
IDR/nterm_analysis.py: MemeConfig dataclass fields are all typed; submit_meme_ebi and poll_meme_ebi have complete annotations

strengths: protein_analysis_utils.py and analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py have complete public API annotations
IDR/nterm_analysis.py public functions are well-typed including dataclasses
Use of float | None and str | None in IDR/idr_analysis.py return types is accurate
type_safety.issue_character: Type annotation gaps are concentrated in IDR/idr_analysis.py internal/private helpers and Shiny server callbacks in app/app.py — public APIs are typed but implementation internals are not.
type_safety.score_rationale: Public APIs in the main utility modules are adequately typed. The gaps are in IDR pipeline internal helpers (_worker_fn, _PredictionWorker methods, bare dict type) and Shiny app callbacks. For a research codebase this is acceptable but leaves the type checker unable to verify the most complex parts of the IDR pipeline. Score is in the lower-middle range.

issues.summary: _aiupred_cache module-level dict is typed as bare 'dict' without generic parameters
evidence: _aiupred_cache: dict = {} — bare dict; keys are str (aiupred_dir paths), values are Optional module objects
Type checker cannot verify key/value types for cache lookups
issues.suggestion: Annotate as _aiupred_cache: dict[str, Any] = {} (or use a more specific type if the module type can be named)

issues.summary: _worker_fn and _PredictionWorker private methods lack return type annotations in an otherwise typed module
evidence: def _worker_fn(job_q, result_q, aiupred_dir: str, mobidb_bindir: str) -> None: — job_q and result_q parameters are untyped
_PredictionWorker._start() and _stop() have no return type; _proc: '_mp.Process | None' uses string annotation
issues.suggestion: Add mp.Queue type annotations to _worker_fn parameters; add -> None return annotations to _start() and _stop()

root_causes: IDR pipeline internal helpers were written for functionality first; type annotations were added to the public API but not to private implementation helpers
